clusters.rationale: Reality testing and wake-back-to-bed are both induction techniques specifically designed to achieve lucid dreaming; they are methods, not distinct phenomena.
Meditation vision is a specific type of visual phenomenon occurring during meditation; meditation experience is the broader category encompassing all altered states during practice.
False awakening is a specific hypnagogic/sleep-transition phenomenon; hypnagogic exploration is the broader practice of cultivating and observing such transitional states.
Gateway Experience is a branded program/technology designed to induce astral projection experiences; the underlying phenomenon is astral projection.
Hemi-Sync and frequency-following-response are specific implementations or neurological mechanisms of binaural beat technology; binaural beats is the parent phenomenon.
Drum journey and group shamanic journey are specific methods/contexts of shamanic journeying; the core phenomenon is shamanic journeying itself.
Pranayama and hyperventilation trance are specific breathing techniques; breathwork is the broader category encompassing structured breathing practices.
Dream incubation is a specific technique for seeking particular dreams; dream recall training is the broader practice of enhancing dream memory and accessibility.

rename_only.rationale: Clinical jargon-heavy phrasing; 'Non-Dual Meditation' is more accessible and how practitioners would search for this.
The word 'training' is redundant; the phenomenon itself is remote viewing, and training is implicit in the practice.

clusters.rationale: Ape Canyon Creature is a regional variant of Bigfoot sightings from Washington state, not a distinct cryptid.
Almas and Almasti are the same Central Asian hominid cryptid, with Almasti being the more commonly used modern name and having higher report count.
All three are regional variants of the same phenomenon: large black felids reported in areas without native big cats (British Isles, Australia).
Beast of Bray Road is a werewolf-like cryptid from Wisconsin that is a regional variant of the broader Dogman phenomenon.
All three are winged humanoid cryptids with glowing eyes; Owlman and Van Meter Visitor are regional variants of the same phenomenon type as Mothman.
Teratorn and Thunderbird both describe enormous prehistoric-style birds with 12-20 foot wingspans; Teratorn is a scientific name for the same phenomenon.
Metoh-kangmi is the original Himalayan name for the same creature known as Yeti; Yeti is the more widely recognized name with higher report count.
Smoke Wolf is an Appalachian folklore variant of shapeshifting entities; the core phenomenon is the same as Skinwalker traditions.
Tennessee Wildman and Fouke Monster are regional variants of the same Appalachian/Southern bipedal hairy humanoid phenomenon.
Both are regional Bigfoot-type creatures (Ohio and Arizona respectively); Grassman has higher report count and is the more established name.
Lake Worth Monster and Pope Lick Monster are both goat-man hybrids; regional variants of the same cryptid type.
Pendulum divination, radiesthesia, and crystal dowsing are all specific forms of dowsing; dowsing is the most general and recognizable term.
Mirror scrying is a specific form of scrying; scrying-and-crystal-gazing covers the broader practice.
Oracle card reading is a contemporary form of divination using cards similar to tarot; both are card-based divination practices.
Ceremonial magic and high magic are synonymous with or subsets of ritual magic; ritual magic has 101 reports and is the most commonly used term.
Sigil magic is a specific operative magical technique; operative magic is the broader category emphasizing practical results-oriented magic.
All shamanic practices (journey, extraction, soul retrieval, power animal work) are specific techniques within shamanism; shamanism is the overarching tradition.
Gardnerian Wicca, Faery Wicca, Esbat practice, Sabbat celebration, and Book of Shadows are all specific Wiccan traditions or practices; Wicca is the parent tradition with 65 reports.
Vodou ritual ceremony and Loa invocation are both core Vodou practices; they represent the same religious tradition.
Hoodoo and Conjure Work are synonymous African American folk magic traditions; conjure work has higher report count (170 vs 63).
Sigil meditation is a specific meditative technique applied to sigil magic; sigil magic is the broader practice with 126 reports.
Séances and mediumship are forms of communication with the deceased, which is the core of necromancy; necromancy is the broader esoteric term.
Wiccan Sabbat celebration is the Wiccan observance of the Wheel of the Year; they refer to the same eight seasonal festivals.
Operator review: sympathetic-magic is the foundational principle; candle-magic and voodoo-dolls are both specific applications. Single merged cluster supersedes Haikus three overlapping clusters.

rename_only.rationale: Overly clinical phrasing; 'Spirit Possession Divination' is more natural.
Overly descriptive; 'Power Animal' is the standard term used in shamanic practice communities.
Overly descriptive; 'Soul Retrieval' is the standard term in shamanic healing.
Overly descriptive; 'Extraction' is the standard term in shamanic healing.
Overly descriptive; 'Umbanda' is the religious tradition name and how practitioners refer to it.
Overly descriptive; 'Spiritual Baptist' is the standard name for this religious tradition.
Overly clinical; 'Heathenry' or 'Ásatrú' is how practitioners refer to this Norse reconstruction tradition.
Overly descriptive; 'Tantra' is the standard term for this Hindu/Buddhist esoteric tradition.
Overly descriptive; 'Unio Mystica' is the established Latin term for this Christian contemplative practice.
Overly clinical phrasing; 'Tobacco Ceremony' is more natural.
Overly clinical; 'Palo Santo Smudging' is how this practice is commonly known.
Overly clinical; 'Pule' is the Hawaiian term and should be foregrounded.
Overly descriptive; 'Bruja' is the standard term for this Latin American witchcraft tradition.
Overly narrow; Obeah encompasses more than just healing practices.
Overly academic; 'Grimoire Magic' is more natural for practitioners.
Overly descriptive; 'Book of Shadows' is the standard term.

clusters.rationale: Both refer to the same phenomenon of localized temperature drops associated with paranormal activity; cold-spot-phenomenon has higher report count (691 vs 66).
Both describe paranormal activity occurring on specific anniversary dates; anniversary-haunting has significantly higher report count (247 vs 35) and is the more established term.
Banshee Wail is a specific manifestation of the Banshee phenomenon; banshee is the canonical entity with higher report count (14 vs 37, but banshee is the primary phenomenon).
Both describe paranormal activity in threshold/transitional spaces; liminal-space-haunting is the broader, more established term with higher report count (869 vs 291).
All three refer to the same urban legend phenomenon of ghostly hitchhikers; phantom-hitchhiker is the most common terminology with 21 reports.
Both describe conscious, responsive spirits; intelligent-haunting is the broader category with much higher report count (10620 vs 1431).
Both refer to supernatural doubles of living persons appearing as death omens; doppelganger is the more widely recognized term with higher report count (732 vs 215).
Portal-haunting describes paranormal activity at dimensional gateways; portal is the broader concept; portal-haunting is more specific with 438 reports vs 266.
Both describe spirits that imitate voices of the living; mimicking-spirit is broader and has higher report count (1548 vs 1205).
All three describe mysterious luminous phenomena associated with death/spirits; ghost-lights is the most general term with highest report count (548).
Both describe physical items with supernatural properties; haunted-object is broader and has higher report count (1481 vs 152).
Both describe spirits claiming ownership of locations; territorial-ghost is broader with much higher report count (1637 vs 52).
All three describe benevolent protective spirits; guardian-spirit is the most general term with highest report count (845).
Both describe repetitive paranormal activity; residual-haunting is the established paranormal terminology with higher report count (2655 vs 165).
Both describe apparitions appearing at moments of death/crisis; crisis-ghost is the established term with much higher report count (3674 vs 163).
Both describe supernatural harbingers of death; death-omen is the broader category with much higher report count (1046 vs 4).
All three describe apparitions of deceased animals; phantom-animal is the most general term with highest report count (1028).
Both describe deceased individuals appearing to living relatives; visitor-spirit is broader with much higher report count (11000 vs 1346).
Both describe spirits unable to move on; trapped-spirit is more commonly used terminology with higher report count (666 vs 156).
Both center on specific deceased individuals; person-based-haunting is broader with higher report count (1011 vs 591).
Both describe luminescent apparitions; glowing-entity is the broader category with much higher report count (856 vs 16).
Both describe fog/mist-like apparitions; smoky-manifestation has higher report count (324 vs 124) and is more established terminology.
Suicide-site-haunting is a specific subset of triggered-haunting; triggered-haunting is the broader category with much higher report count (12431 vs 288).
Both describe entities bonding to persons/locations; attachment-entity is broader and has much higher report count (10385 vs 130).
Both describe malevolent entity manifestations; demonic-haunting is the broader category with higher report count (2805 vs 1563).
Operator review: trapped-spirit has higher report count (666 vs 156). Replaces Haikus reciprocal pair confusion.
Operator review: apparition is the broader more established term (2812 reports vs 705). Inverted from Haikus original which had full-body-apparition canonical.

rename_only.rationale: Current name is overly clinical and location-specific; proposed name is more accessible and describes the phenomenon generically.
Current name is generic and could apply to many contexts; proposed name is more specific to paranormal/spiritual practice.
Current name is overly clinical; proposed name reflects how people actually refer to this spirit communication method.
Current name lacks paranormal context; proposed name clarifies this is a spirit communication method.
Current name is jargon-heavy; proposed name is more accessible to general audiences.
Current name is jargon-heavy; proposed name clarifies the communication aspect.
While not overly clinical, 'Ghost Photography' is more commonly used by paranormal enthusiasts and the general public.
Current name is overly clinical; proposed name adds paranormal context.
Current name is awkwardly phrased; proposed name more naturally describes spirits expressing confusion about their state.
Current name is overly long and jargon-heavy; proposed name is more concise while retaining meaning.

clusters.rationale: Ancestral communication is a specific cultural practice of the broader after-death communication phenomenon; both involve contact with deceased individuals.
Psychic empathy is a subset of clairsentience—both describe sensing emotions and states of others through paranormal means.
Psychic smell and clairalience both describe perceiving odors without physical source; clairalience is the established parapsychological term.
Precognitive dreams and premonitions are specific manifestations of the broader precognition ability; all involve knowledge of future events.
Psychokinetic effects and RSPK are specific categories or theories of psychokinesis; all describe mind-matter interaction.
Etheric projection is distinguished from astral travel only by proximity to physical plane; both describe consciousness separation from body.
Energy medicine, distant healing, and psychic healing are all specific modalities or applications of energy healing practices.
Channeling is a form of mediumship; both involve receiving and transmitting messages from non-physical entities.
Dream telepathy, telephone telepathy, and telepathic bonds are specific contexts or manifestations of the broader telepathy phenomenon.
Both describe transitional sleep-wake states; hypnagogic is sleep onset, hypnopompic is sleep offset—complementary aspects of the same phenomenon.
Psychic self-defense and cord cutting are specific techniques within the broader category of psychic protection practices.
Aura cleansing presupposes aura perception; cleansing is a practice applied to perceived auras, not a distinct phenomenon.

rename_only.rationale: EVP is the standard abbreviation used by researchers and the public; more searchable and recognizable.
Clinical phrasing; 'expanded consciousness' is more commonly used in paranormal and spiritual contexts.
Minor: 'phenomena' is clinical; 'experiences' aligns with how witnesses describe these events.
Minor: 'phenomena' is clinical; 'experiences' aligns with how witnesses describe these events.
Simpler, more natural; how people actually search for and discuss these objects.
Simpler; 'psychic' is redundant in paranormal context.
Simpler; 'psychic' is redundant in paranormal context.
More natural phrasing; 'states' better captures the transitional nature of these experiences.
Tulpa is the more recognizable term in paranormal communities; thoughtform is overly clinical.

clusters.rationale: Both entries describe the same psychological phenomenon of false familiarity; d-j-vu is a duplicate with higher report count (147 vs 76).
All three describe transcendent states of expanded consciousness and unity; cosmic-consciousness is the established psychological term with clearest definition.
Astral experiences and sudden OBEs are both subcategories of OBE phenomena; sudden-obe is a specific type (spontaneous OBE) and astral-experiences is the same underlying mechanism.
All three describe transitional consciousness states between different modes; boundary-states is the most established term with highest report count (495).
Mandela Effect is a specific collective manifestation of false memory syndrome; both involve false shared memories but Mandela Effect is a distinct phenomenon (collective vs individual).

rename_only.rationale: Overly clinical phrasing; 'end-of-life experience' is how people naturally refer to this phenomenon.
The summary itself notes the alternative name 'Frequency Illusion' which is more intuitive and searchable than the obscure historical reference.
Remove unnecessary 'The' article for cleaner, more searchable naming convention.
Overly clinical jargon; 'mass psychosis' is the more common colloquial term people use to search for this concept.
